reasoning: On the first resolution date (Nov 16, 2025), Wikipedia will still reflect the official FIDE ratings from Nov 1, which list Caruana's rating at 2795. Since this is identical to the rating on Nov 9, the increase is 0% (less than the required 1%). Thus, the probability for Nov 16 is flatly minimal. To be 1% higher than 2795, his rating must reach at least 2823. Gaining 28 classical rating points requires an incredibly rare and massive outperformance (winning +7 or +8 more games than expected against top competition). Furthermore, December and January are generally low-volume months for classical chess, with the primary event being Tata Steel in January. Even an historic, first-place finish at Tata Steel would likely gain fewer than 20 points. Given his age and the extreme stability of 2800+ ratings, hitting 2823 by Feb 2026 is extremely unlikely. Uncertainty increases slightly with a longer time horizon, yielding estimated probabilities of 2%, 3%, and 6%.
